text.title: Solo Blameless Postmortem Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Solo Blameless Postmortem Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Postmortem uses 'the system / the process' framing; no 'I should have' / 'the user broke'.
rule.rationale: Postmortem uses 'the system / the process' framing; no 'I should have' / 'the user broke'.

rule.statement: Artefact has exactly five sections: incident summary, timeline, root cause, lessons, prevention rule.
rule.rationale: Artefact has exactly five sections: incident summary, timeline, root cause, lessons, prevention rule.

rule.statement: Prevention rule names a concrete observable (file, threshold, gate) — not vague advice.
rule.rationale: Prevention rule names a concrete observable (file, threshold, gate) — not vague advice.

rule.statement: Prevention rule is appended to memory/mistakes.md and a derived pattern to memory/patterns.md.
rule.rationale: Prevention rule is appended to memory/mistakes.md and a derived pattern to memory/patterns.md.

rule.statement: Postmortem authoring time is capped at 20 minutes; deeper analysis is a separate task.
rule.rationale: Postmortem authoring time is capped at 20 minutes; deeper analysis is a separate task.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-solo-blameless-postmortem-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
